target.name: Long Wall
target.founder: Dan Piemont (CEO; ex-SpaceX, ex-Morgan Stanley), Harry O'Hanley (ex-SpaceX, ex-Morgan Stanley)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $544M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Long Wall is a late-stage defense and security company founded by operators with prior experience at SpaceX and Morgan Stanley. The target sector encompasses 10 identifiable peer companies (2 public, 8 private), including Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI, though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage comparables for reliable median benchmarking.

The analysis applies stage-progression methodology, using the $544M in total capital raised as a proxy baseline. A 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $653M. The peer-median approach is not applicable: while 19 same-sector companies exist, filtering for stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints, making a sectoral median unreliable and risking material misstatement.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $555M to $751M, with $653M as the median point. This represents a modest 20% uplift from deployed capital, consistent with late-stage risk and typical Series C-D step patterns. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-cohort comparables in Defense & Security at this scale and stage, limiting cross-validation of the stage-progression result. Management pedigree and sector tailwinds are noted but do not alter the quantitative framework.
